subject: english
title: Unit 9 Grammar 1 — Cause and effect with double comparatives
subtitle: v1 · Leo's Science Fun Park spine · 24 slides · two patterns (the more..., the more... / the more..., the -er...) · comma rule · LP-verbatim Practice + Be the Expert · park grand-opening payoff

content: Form double comparatives to show that one action causes another.
Join two short sentences into one double-comparative sentence.
Recognize the comma that always separates the cause from the effect.
language: Use the pattern: The more + subject + verb, the more + subject + verb.
Use the pattern: The more + subject + verb, comparative adjective + subject + verb.
Change adjectives into their comparative form (fast → the faster, good → the better, high → the higher).

referenceLinks.label: Cause and effect with double comparatives
